question: Is the background recognizable as the street corner from the first input image, maintaining its original anime style?
Are both the bicycle and the robot present in the generated image?
Is the bicycle positioned parked at the roadside (near the curb or building edge)?
Is the robot positioned standing beside the bicycle?
Is the bicycle recognizable as the vintage model from the second input image (e.g., leather saddle, drop handlebars, thin tires)?
Is the robot recognizable as the specific character from the third input image (e.g., white round body, screen face, wheeled base)?
Do both the bicycle and the robot have thick, clear outlines consistent with the line art of the anime background?
Are the realistic textures of the bicycle (e.g., rust, metallic noise) simplified into clean, flat color blocks?
Are the 3D-render traits of the robot (e.g., glossy reflections, smooth gradients) replaced with flat anime-style coloring?
Are the shadows on the bicycle and robot rendered as flat cel-shaded tiers rather than smooth realistic gradients?
Are there defined, anime-style contact shadows on the ground beneath the bicycle tires and the robot (flat shapes, not soft realistic shadows)?
Are the bicycle and robot scaled appropriately relative to each other and the surrounding buildings?
Does the perspective of the bicycle and robot match the vanishing points and depth of the street scene?
Do the bicycle and robot appear firmly grounded on the pavement, without floating or clipping into the ground?
Is the bicycle free from severe structural distortions (e.g., warped wheels, disconnected frame, impossible geometry)?
Is the robot free from severe structural distortions (e.g., malformed limbs, asymmetrical body shape, missing wheels)?
Is the image free from blending artifacts (e.g., white halos, jagged cutouts) where the added objects meet the background?
Is the overall image free from photorealistic elements or spurious duplications of objects?
